name: sca_full_recovery_storm
description: Simulates the worst-case SCA recovery scenario: all agents simultaneously perform ModuleFull with maximum check count.
notes: WORST CASE: 500 agents x 600 checks = 300,000 DataValues competing for WorkersQueue.
Production trigger: Manager restart after maintenance → all agents detect
indexer data is stale (checksum mismatch) → all trigger performRecovery()
→ synchronizeDatabaseSnapshot() loads ALL checks into m_inMemoryData
Agent-side: Each agent has ~9-50 MB in m_inMemoryData.
Not directly measurable from this benchmark, but the manager effect is:
- WorkersQueue: 300k msgs x 4 KB = ~1.2 GB if unbounded.
- IndexerQueue: 500 Response{} objects (one per completed session).
- Each completed session triggers deleteByQuery for the FULL mode
(delete old data) + bulkIndex (new data): 2x indexer load.
Key difference from sca_burst (200 agents): 2.5x more concurrent sessions
pushes closer to maxSessions limit and exercises the session-limit
at this scale. The goal is to measure degradation, not pass/fail.
